quality.rationale: Requires multi-step spinor-tensor index manipulation, symmetric/antisymmetric decomposition, trace extraction, and Hodge duality identification

origin.question: Problem 21.16. Let $\mathbf{T_{ab}} = \mathbf{T_{AA'B'B'}}$ in a spinor representation. What tensor corresponds to $\mathbf{T_{BA'AB'}}$ ?
origin.solution: Solution 21.16. Since primed and unprimed indices commute, we have$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {T}_{\mathrm {(a b)}} = \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathbf {T}_{\mathbf {A B A^{\prime} B^{\prime}}} + \mathbf {T}_{\mathbf {B A B^{\prime} A^{\prime}}}\right) \\= \frac {1}{4} \left(\mathrm {T_{A B A^{'} B^{'}} - T_{B A A^{'} B^{'}} + T_{B A B^{'} A^{'}} - T_{A B B^{'} A^{'}}}\right) \\+ \frac {1}{2} \mathrm {\Delta T_{(a b)}} + \frac {1}{4} \left(\mathrm {\Delta T_{B A A^{'} B^{'}}} + \mathrm {\Delta T_{A B B^{'} A^{'}}}\right) . \\\end{array}$$With the result of Problem 21.14, this can be written as$$\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {T}_{\left(\mathrm {a b}\right)} = 2 \mathrm {T}_{\left[ \mathrm {A B}\right] \left[ \mathrm {A}^{\prime} \mathrm {B}^{\prime}\right]} + \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {B A A}^{\prime} \mathrm {B}^{\prime}} + \mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {A B B}^{\prime} \mathrm {A}^{\prime}}\right) \\= \frac {1}{2} \varepsilon_{\mathbf {A B}} \varepsilon_{\mathbf {A^{\prime} B^{\prime}}} \mathrm {T_{C}}_{\mathbf {C^{\prime}}}^{\mathbf {C^{\prime}}} + \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathrm {T_{B A A^{\prime} B^{\prime}}} + \mathrm {T_{A B B^{\prime} A^{\prime}}}\right). \\\end{array}$$Finally, using the result of Problem 21.15, we get$$\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {B A A^{\prime} B^{\prime}}} = \mathrm {T}_{(\mathrm {a b})} - \frac {1}{2} \varepsilon_{\mathrm {A B}} \varepsilon_{\mathrm {A^{\prime} B^{\prime}}} \mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {C}}^{\mathrm {C}} \mathrm {C}^{\prime} + * \mathrm {T}_{\mathrm {a b}} \\\mathbf {\tau} = \mathbf {\tau}_{(\mathbf {a b})} - \frac {1}{2} \mathbf {\tau}_{\mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {a b}}} \mathbf {\tau}_{\mathbf {c}}^{\mathbf {c}} + \mathbf {i} * \mathbf {T}_{\mathbf {a b}}. \\\end{array}$$
